𐤉𐤄𐤅𐤄

THE ANCIENT COVENANT RECORD

Volume Ten — Part Three

The Book of Yehoshua

YEHOSHUA

The Conquest of the Land

Chapters 16 through 24

The Tribal Allotments — The Cities of Refuge — The Covenant at Shekhem

PRIMARY MANUSCRIPT AUTHORITIES

4QJosha (4Q47) — Cave 4 — Primary DSS witness for Yehoshua

4QJoshb (4Q48) — Cave 4 — Supporting DSS fragments

Ethiopian Orit Ge'ez — Complete Canon — Yehoshua preserved in full

YHWH and The Creator only — no substitute titles

COMPARATIVE NOTES AT END OF EVERY CHAPTER

DSS · ORIT GE'EZ · MASORETIC VARIANT · CRITICAL NOTE

YHWH / 𐤉𐤄𐤅𐤄 and The Creator only — no substitute titles

All personal names in original Hebrew form throughout

YEHOSHUA — CHAPTERS 16 THROUGH 24 — ALLOTMENTS AND FAREWELL

YEHOSHUA — CHAPTER 16 The Allotment of Efrayim — The Kena'anites Dwell Among Them

1 The allotment of the people of Yosef went from the Yarden by Yerikho, east of the waters of Yerikho, into the wilderness, going up from Yerikho into the hill country to Beit-El.

2 Then going from Beit-El to Luz, it passes along to Atarot, the territory of the Arkhites.

3 Then it goes down westward to the territory of the Yafletites, as far as the territory of Lower Beit-Kharon, then to Gezer, and it ends at the sea.

4 The people of Yosef, Menasheh and Efrayim, received their inheritance.

5 The territory of the people of Efrayim by their clans was as follows: the boundary of their inheritance on the east was Atrot-Addar as far as Upper Beit-Kharon,

6 And the boundary goes from there to the sea. On the north is Mikhmetat. Then on the east the boundary turns around toward Tana'at-Shiloh, and passes along beyond it on the east to Yanoakh,

7 Then it goes down from Yanoakh to Atarot and to Na'arah, and touches Yerikho, ending at the Yarden.

8 The boundary goes from Tappuakh westward to the brook Kanah, and ends at the sea. Such is the inheritance of the tribe of the people of Efrayim by their clans,

9 Together with the towns that were set apart for the people of Efrayim within the inheritance of the people of Menasheh, all those towns with their villages.

10 However, they did not drive out the Kena'anites who lived in Gezer, so the Kena'anites have lived in the midst of Efrayim to this day but have been made to do forced labor.

COMPARATIVE MANUSCRIPT NOTES

[DSS] 4QJosha (4Q47) preserves fragmentary portions of Yehoshua. Chapter 16 opens the allotment of the two tribes of Yosef — Efrayim and Menasheh. Verse 10 repeats the pattern of incomplete conquest: the Kena'anites of Gezer were not driven out and remain to this day.

[ORIT GE'EZ] The Orit Ge'ez preserves Chapter 16 in full. The allotment of Yosef's two tribes — Efrayim and Menasheh — fulfills the double portion promised to Yosef through his father Yaakov in Bereshit 48:5-6, where Yaakov adopted both sons as his own.

[MASORETIC VARIANT] No significant variants from the DSS in Yehoshua Chapter 16. The Masoretic text aligns with the DSS reading. Verse 10 — the Kena'anites made to do forced labor — parallels the Giv'onites' fate in Chapter 9. Incomplete obedience to the herem produces coexistence rather than covenant separation.

[CRITICAL NOTE] Verse 10 — they did not drive out the Kena'anites who lived in Gezer. The pattern of incomplete conquest that begins here in Efrayim's territory will be repeated across the remaining allotments. YHWH commanded the complete driving out of the nations — Devarim 7:1-2. The failure to complete the covenant commission does not produce neutrality — it produces the ongoing corruption of covenant faithfulness that the book of Shofetim will document in detail. Every instance of they did not drive out is a seed of future covenant failure.

YEHOSHUA — CHAPTER 17 The Allotment of Menasheh — The Daughters of Tzelofkhad — Too Many People for One Lot

1 Then allotment was made to the tribe of Menasheh, for he was the firstborn of Yosef. To Makhir the firstborn of Menasheh, the father of Gil'ad, were allotted Gil'ad and Bashan, because he was a man of war.

2 And allotments were made to the rest of the people of Menasheh by their clans, Avi-ezer, Khelek, Asri'el, Shekhem, Khefer, and Shemida. These were the male descendants of Menasheh the son of Yosef, by their clans.

3 Now Tzelofkhad the son of Khefer, son of Gil'ad, son of Makhir, son of Menasheh, had no sons, but only daughters, and these are the names of his daughters: Makhlah, No'ah, Khoglah, Milkah, and Tirtzah.

4 They approached Ele'azar the priest and Yehoshua the son of Nun and the leaders and said, YHWH commanded Moshe to give us an inheritance among our brothers. So according to the mouth of YHWH he gave them an inheritance among the brothers of their father.

5 Thus there fell to Menasheh ten portions, besides the land of Gil'ad and Bashan, which is on the other side of the Yarden,

6 Because the daughters of Menasheh received an inheritance along with his sons. The land of Gil'ad was allotted to the rest of the people of Menasheh.

7 The territory of Menasheh reached from Asher to Mikhmetat, which is east of Shekhem. Then the boundary goes along southward to the inhabitants of En-Tappuakh.

8 The land of Tappuakh belonged to Menasheh, but the town of Tappuakh on the boundary of Menasheh belonged to the people of Efrayim.

9 Then the boundary goes down to the brook Kanah. These cities, to the south of the brook, among the cities of Menasheh, belong to Efrayim. The boundary of Menasheh goes along the north side of the brook and ends at the sea.

10 The land to the south is Efrayim's and that to the north is Menasheh's, with the sea forming its boundary. On the north Asher is reached, and on the east Yissaskhar.

11 Also in Yissaskhar and in Asher, Menasheh had Beit-She'an and its villages, Yivle'am and its villages, the inhabitants of Dor and its villages, the inhabitants of En-Dor and its villages, the inhabitants of Ta'anakh and its villages, and the inhabitants of Megiddo and its villages — the third is Naphot.

12 Yet the people of Menasheh could not take possession of those cities, but the Kena'anites persisted in dwelling in that land.

13 When the people of Yisra'EL grew strong, they put the Kena'anites to forced labor, but did not utterly drive them out.

14 Then the people of Yosef spoke to Yehoshua, saying, Why have you given me but one lot and one portion as an inheritance, although I am a numerous people, since all along YHWH has blessed me?

15 And Yehoshua said to them, If you are a numerous people, go up by yourselves to the forest, and there clear ground for yourselves in the land of the Perizzites and the Refa'im, since the hill country of Efrayim is too narrow for you.

16 The people of Yosef said, The hill country is not enough for us. Yet all the Kena'anites who dwell in the plain have chariots of iron, both those in Beit-She'an and its villages and those in the Valley of Yizre'el.

17 Then Yehoshua said to the house of Yosef, to Efrayim and Menasheh, You are a numerous people and have great power. You shall not have one allotment only,

18 But the hill country shall be yours, for though it is a forest, you shall clear it and possess it to its farthest borders. For you shall drive out the Kena'anites, though they have chariots of iron, and though they are strong.

COMPARATIVE MANUSCRIPT NOTES

[DSS] 4QJosha (4Q47) preserves fragmentary portions of Chapter 17. The case of Tzelofkhad's daughters — verses 3-6 — confirms the covenant ruling of Bamidbar 27:1-11, where YHWH commanded that daughters inherit when there are no sons. Yehoshua honors the Mosaic ruling without hesitation.

[ORIT GE'EZ] The Orit Ge'ez preserves Chapter 17 in full. The daughters of Tzelofkhad appealing to the covenant ruling and receiving their father's inheritance — verse 4 — is read as the covenant principle that YHWH's word spoken through Moshe is binding upon every generation and every leader.

[MASORETIC VARIANT] No significant variants from the DSS in Yehoshua Chapter 17. The Masoretic text aligns with the DSS reading. Verse 12-13 — the Kena'anites persisted — they were put to forced labor but not driven out — again repeats the pattern of incomplete covenant obedience.

[CRITICAL NOTE] Verse 14-18 — the people of Yosef complain that their allotment is too small for their numbers. Yehoshua's response is covenant challenge rather than covenant accommodation: if you are numerous, go clear the forest — drive out the Kena'anites though they have iron chariots. The complaint is answered not with more territory but with the call to take what has already been given. The iron chariots are not an excuse — verse 18 — you shall drive them out though they are strong. Covenant obedience does not wait for favorable military conditions.

YEHOSHUA — CHAPTER 18 The Tent of Meeting at Shiloh — Seven Tribes Receive Their Allotments — The Allotment of Binyamin

1 Then the whole congregation of the people of Yisra'EL assembled at Shiloh and set up the tent of meeting there. The land lay subdued before them.

2 There remained among the people of Yisra'EL seven tribes whose inheritance had not yet been apportioned.

3 So Yehoshua said to the people of Yisra'EL, How long will you put off going in to take possession of the land, which YHWH, the Creator of your fathers, has given you?

4 Provide three men from each tribe, and I will send them out that they may set out and go up and down the land. They shall write a description of it with a view to their inheritances, and then come to me.

5 They shall divide it into seven portions. Yehudah shall continue in his territory on the south, and the house of Yosef shall continue in their territory on the north.

6 And you shall describe the land in seven portions and bring the description here to me. And I will cast lots for you here before YHWH our Creator.

7 The Levites have no portion among you, for the priesthood of YHWH is their heritage. And Gad and Reuven and half the tribe of Menasheh have received their inheritance beyond the Yarden eastward, which Moshe the servant of YHWH gave them.

8 So the men arose and went, and Yehoshua charged those who went to write the description of the land, saying, Go up and down in the land and write a description and return to me. And I will cast lots for you here before YHWH in Shiloh.

9 So the men went and passed up and down in the land and wrote in a book a description of it by towns in seven divisions. Then they came to Yehoshua to the camp at Shiloh,

10 And Yehoshua cast lots for them in Shiloh before YHWH. And there Yehoshua apportioned the land to the people of Yisra'EL, to each his portion.

11 The lot of the tribe of the people of Binyamin according to its clans came up, and the territory allotted to it fell between the people of Yehudah and the people of Yosef.

12 On the north side their boundary began at the Yarden. Then the boundary goes up to the shoulder north of Yerikho, then up through the hill country westward, and it ends at the wilderness of Beit-Aven.

13 From there the boundary passes along southward in the direction of Luz, to the shoulder of Luz — the same is Beit-El — then the boundary goes down to Atarot-Addar, on the mountain that lies south of Lower Beit-Kharon.

14 Then the boundary goes in another direction, turning on the western side southward from the mountain that lies to the south, opposite Beit-Kharon, and it ends at Kiryat-Ba'al — that is, Kiryat-Ye'arim — a city belonging to the people of Yehudah. This forms the western side.

15 And the southern side begins at the outskirts of Kiryat-Ye'arim. And the boundary goes from there westward, and it goes to the spring of the waters of Neftokha.

16 Then the boundary goes down to the border of the mountain that overlooks the Valley of the Son of Hinnom, which is at the north end of the Valley of Refa'im. And it then goes down the Valley of Hinnom, south of the shoulder of the Yevusites, and downward to En-Rogel.

17 Then it bends in a northerly direction going to En-Shemesh, and from there goes to Gelilot, which is opposite the ascent of Adummim. Then it goes down to the stone of Bohan the son of Reuven.

18 Passing along north of the shoulder of Beit-Aravah, it goes down to the Aravah.

19 Then the boundary passes along to the north of the shoulder of Beit-Khoglah. And the boundary ends at the northern bay of the Salt Sea, at the south end of the Yarden. This is the southern boundary.

20 The Yarden forms its boundary on the eastern side. This is the inheritance of the people of Binyamin, according to their clans, boundary by boundary all around.

21 Now the cities of the tribe of the people of Binyamin according to their clans were Yerikho, Beit-Khoglah, Emek-Ketzitz,

22 Beit-Aravah, Tzemarayim, Beit-El,

23 Avvim, Parah, Ofrah,

24 Kefar-Ammonai, Ofni, and Geva — twelve cities with their villages.

25 Giv'on, Ramah, Be'erot,

26 Mitzpeh, Kefira, Moza,

27 Rekem, Irpe'el, Tar'alah,

28 Tzela, Elef, Yevus — that is, Yerushalayim — Giv'at, and Kiryat — fourteen cities with their villages. This is the inheritance of the people of Binyamin according to their clans.

COMPARATIVE MANUSCRIPT NOTES

[DSS] 4QJosha (4Q47) preserves fragmentary portions of Chapter 18. The assembly at Shiloh — verse 1 — is the first mention of Shiloh as the covenant assembly point. The tent of meeting is set up there, establishing Shiloh as the central sanctuary before Yerushalayim. Shiloh will remain the covenant center through the period of Shofetim and into Shemu'el.

[ORIT GE'EZ] The Orit Ge'ez preserves Chapter 18 in full. Yehoshua's rebuke in verse 3 — how long will you put off going in to take possession of the land — is read as the covenant challenge to covenant passivity: YHWH has given the land, but the people must act to take it.

[MASORETIC VARIANT] No significant variants from the DSS in Yehoshua Chapter 18. The Masoretic text aligns with the DSS reading. The casting of lots before YHWH at Shiloh — verse 10 — is the covenant method of territory allocation: the outcome is YHWH's determination, not human negotiation.

[CRITICAL NOTE] Verse 3 — how long will you put off going in to take possession of the land? Seven tribes have not yet taken their inheritance. The covenant gift is waiting but the covenant people are passive. Yehoshua's question is not rhetorical — it is the same covenant challenge as YHWH's charge to Yehoshua in Chapter 1: arise and go. The covenant inheritance does not come by waiting. It comes by acting on the promise already given. The survey, the description, the lots — all of this is the covenant process of claiming what YHWH has already declared given.

YEHOSHUA — CHAPTER 19 The Allotments of Shim'on, Zevulun, Yissaskhar, Asher, Naftali, Dan — Yehoshua Receives His Inheritance

1 The second lot came out for Shim'on, for the tribe of the people of Shim'on, according to their clans, and their inheritance was in the midst of the inheritance of the people of Yehudah.

2 And they had for their inheritance Be'er-Sheva, Sheva, Moladah,

3 Khattzar-Shu'al, Balah, Etzem,

4 Eltolad, Betul, Khormah,

5 Tziklag, Beit-Markabot, Khattzar-Susah,

6 Beit-Levavot, and Sharukhен — thirteen cities with their villages.

7 Ayin, Rimmon, Ether, and Ashan — four cities with their villages —

8 Together with all the villages around these cities as far as Ba'alat-Be'er, Ramat of the Negev. This was the inheritance of the tribe of the people of Shim'on according to their clans.

9 The inheritance of the people of Shim'on formed part of the territory of the people of Yehudah. Because the portion of the people of Yehudah was too large for them, the people of Shim'on obtained an inheritance in the midst of their inheritance.

10 The third lot came up for the people of Zevulun, according to their clans. And the territory of their inheritance reached as far as Sarid.

11 Then their boundary goes up westward and on to Mar'alah and touches Dabbesheth, then the brook that is east of Yokne'am.

12 From Sarid it goes in the other direction eastward toward the sunrise to the boundary of Kislot-Tavor. From there it goes to Daberath, then up to Yafiya.

13 From there it passes along on the east toward the sunrise to Gat-Khefer, to Ittah-Katzin, and going on to Rimmon it bends toward Neah,

14 Then on the north the boundary turns about to Khannathon, and it ends at the Valley of Yiftakh-El —

15 Kattat, Nahalal, Shimron, Idalah, and Beit-Lekhem — twelve cities with their villages.

16 This is the inheritance of the people of Zevulun, according to their clans — these cities with their villages.

17 The fourth lot came out for Yissaskhar, for the people of Yissaskhar, according to their clans.

18 Their territory included Yizre'el, Kesullot, Shunem,

19 Khafarayim, Shion, Anakharat,

20 Rabbith, Kishyon, Evetz,

21 Remmet, En-Gannim, En-Khaddah, Beit-Patztzetz.

22 The boundary also touches Tavor, Shahatzumah, and Beit-Shemesh, and their boundary ends at the Yarden — sixteen cities with their villages.

23 This is the inheritance of the tribe of the people of Yissaskhar, according to their clans — the cities with their villages.

24 The fifth lot came out for the tribe of the people of Asher according to their clans.

25 Their territory included Khelkat, Khali, Beten, Akhshaph,

26 Alammelekh, Amad, and Mishal. On the west it touches Karmel and Shikhор-Livnat.

27 Then it turns eastward, goes to Beit-Dagon, and touches Zevulun and the Valley of Yiftakh-El northward to Beit-Emek and Ne'i'el. Then it continues in the north to Kavul,

28 Evron, Rekhov, Khamon, Kanah, as far as Great Tzidon.

29 Then the boundary turns to Ramah, reaching to the fortified city of Tzor. Then the boundary turns to Khosah, and it ends at the sea — Akhdav,

30 Ummah, Afek, and Rekhov — twenty-two cities with their villages.

31 This is the inheritance of the tribe of the people of Asher according to their clans — these cities with their villages.

32 The sixth lot came out for the people of Naftali, for the people of Naftali, according to their clans.

33 And their boundary ran from Khelef, from the oak in Za'anannim, and Adami-Nekev, and Yavenl, as far as Lakkum, and it ended at the Yarden.

34 Then the boundary turns westward to Aznot-Tavor and goes from there to Khukok, touching Zevulun at the south and Asher on the west and Yehudah on the east at the Yarden.

35 The fortified cities are Tziddim, Tzer, Khamath, Rakkat, Kinnereth,

36 Adamah, Ramah, Khattzor,

37 Kedesh, Edre'i, En-Khattzor,

38 Yiron, Migdal-El, Khorem, Beit-Anat, and Beit-Shemesh — nineteen cities with their villages.

39 This is the inheritance of the tribe of the people of Naftali according to their clans — the cities with their villages.

40 The seventh lot came out for the tribe of the people of Dan, according to their clans.

41 And the territory of its inheritance included Tzorah, Eshtaol, Ir-Shemesh,

42 Sha'alavvin, Ayalon, Yitlah,

43 Elon, Timnah, Ekron,

44 Eltekeh, Gibbeton, Ba'alat,

45 Yehud, Benei-Berak, Gat-Rimmon,

46 Me-Yarkon, and Rakkon, with the territory over against Yafo.

47 When the territory of the people of Dan was lost to them, the people of Dan went up and fought against Leshem, and after capturing it and striking it with the sword they took possession of it and settled in it, calling Leshem, Dan, after the name of Dan their ancestor.

48 This is the inheritance of the tribe of the people of Dan, according to their clans — these cities with their villages.

49 When they had finished distributing the several territories of the land as inheritances, the people of Yisra'EL gave an inheritance among them to Yehoshua the son of Nun.

50 By command of YHWH they gave him the city that he asked, Timnat-Serakha in the hill country of Efrayim. And he rebuilt the city and settled in it.

51 These are the inheritances that Ele'azar the priest and Yehoshua the son of Nun and the heads of the fathers' houses of the tribes of the people of Yisra'EL distributed by lot at Shiloh before YHWH, at the entrance of the tent of meeting. So they finished dividing the land.

COMPARATIVE MANUSCRIPT NOTES

[DSS] 4QJosha (4Q47) preserves fragmentary portions of Chapter 19. The chapter completes the seven-tribe allotment and closes with Yehoshua receiving his own city — Timnat-Serakha — as his personal inheritance, given last after all others have received theirs.

[ORIT GE'EZ] The Orit Ge'ez preserves Chapter 19 in full. Verse 49-50 — Yehoshua receives his inheritance last of all, after every tribe has been provided for — is read as the covenant model of servant leadership: the leader takes his portion only after the people have received theirs.

[MASORETIC VARIANT] No significant variants from the DSS in Yehoshua Chapter 19. The Masoretic text aligns with the DSS reading. Verse 47 — the territory of Dan was lost to them — anticipates the fuller account in Shofetim 18 where Dan fails to hold their allotted territory and migrates north.

[CRITICAL NOTE] Verse 49-50 — the people of Yisra'EL gave an inheritance to Yehoshua — by command of YHWH they gave him the city that he asked. Yehoshua receives his inheritance by his own request — not by lot, not by assignment, but by asking. And he receives it last. The leader who distributed the land to every tribe takes his portion only when the covenant work is complete. The city he chooses is in the hill country of Efrayim — his own tribal territory. He rebuilds and settles it. The conqueror ends not in a palace but in a rebuilt city in his own tribe's hills.

THE CITIES OF REFUGE AND LEVITICAL CITIES — CHAPTERS 20 THROUGH 21

YEHOSHUA — CHAPTER 20 The Cities of Refuge — For the Manslayer Who Kills Without Intent — Until He Stands Before the Congregation

1 Then YHWH said to Yehoshua,

2 Say to the people of Yisra'EL, Appoint the cities of refuge, of which I spoke to you through Moshe,

3 That the manslayer who strikes any person without intent or unknowingly may flee there. They shall be for you a refuge from the avenger of blood.

4 He shall flee to one of these cities and shall stand at the entrance of the gate of the city and explain his case to the elders of that city. Then they shall take him into the city and give him a place, and he shall remain with them.

5 And if the avenger of blood pursues him, they shall not give up the manslayer into his hand, because he struck his neighbor unknowingly, and did not hate him in the past.

6 And he shall remain in that city until he has stood before the congregation for judgment, until the death of him who is high priest at the time. Then the manslayer may return to his own town and his own home, to the town from which he fled.

7 So they set apart Kedesh in Galil in the hill country of Naftali, and Shekhem in the hill country of Efrayim, and Kiryat-Arba — that is, Khevron — in the hill country of Yehudah.

8 And beyond the Yarden east of Yerikho, they appointed Betzer in the wilderness on the tableland, from the tribe of Reuven, and Ramot in Gil'ad, from the tribe of Gad, and Golan in Bashan, from the tribe of Menasheh.

9 These were the cities designated for all the people of Yisra'EL and for the stranger sojourning among them, that anyone who killed a person without intent could flee there, so that he might not die by the hand of the avenger of blood, till he stood before the congregation.

COMPARATIVE MANUSCRIPT NOTES

[DSS] 4QJosha (4Q47) preserves fragmentary portions of Chapter 20. The cities of refuge fulfill YHWH's command in Bamidbar 35:9-34. The six cities — three west of the Yarden, three east — provide covenant protection for the unintentional killer, distinguishing manslaughter from murder with a legal precision that is remarkable in the ancient world.

[ORIT GE'EZ] The Orit Ge'ez preserves Chapter 20 in full. The extension of the cities of refuge to the stranger sojourning among Yisra'EL — verse 9 — is read as the covenant principle of equal protection: the covenant legal system protects the sojourner on the same terms as the native Israelite.

[MASORETIC VARIANT] No significant variants from the DSS in Yehoshua Chapter 20. The Masoretic text aligns with the DSS reading. The release of the manslayer upon the death of the high priest — verse 6 — is a unique covenant legal institution: the high priest's life serves as a kind of covenant surety for those sheltered under the cities of refuge.

[CRITICAL NOTE] Verse 3 — that the manslayer who strikes any person without intent or unknowingly may flee there. The covenant legal system of the cities of refuge distinguishes intention from action. The same physical act — killing a person — has entirely different covenant consequences depending on whether it was intentional or not. This is a sophisticated legal principle: culpability requires both the act and the intent. Verse 6 — until he has stood before the congregation for judgment, until the death of him who is high priest. The manslayer is not imprisoned — he lives in the city, in community. The constraint is territorial, not punitive. The death of the high priest functions as a covenant atonement that releases even the unintentional killer to return home.

YEHOSHUA — CHAPTER 21 The Cities of the Levites — Forty-Eight Cities — Not One Word of All YHWH's Good Promises Failed

1 Then the heads of the fathers' houses of the Levites came to Ele'azar the priest and to Yehoshua the son of Nun and to the heads of the fathers' houses of the tribes of the people of Yisra'EL.

2 And they said to them at Shiloh in the land of Kena'an, YHWH commanded through Moshe that we be given cities to dwell in, along with their pasturelands for our livestock.

3 So by command of YHWH the people of Yisra'EL gave to the Levites the following cities and pasturelands out of their inheritance.

4 The lot came out for the clans of the Kohathites. And the Levites who were descendants of Aharon the priest received by lot from the tribes of Yehudah, Shim'on, and Binyamin, thirteen cities.

5 And the rest of the Kohathites received by lot from the clans of the tribe of Efrayim, from the tribe of Dan, and the half-tribe of Menasheh, ten cities.

6 The Gershonites received by lot from the clans of the tribe of Yissaskhar, from the tribe of Asher, from the tribe of Naftali, and from the half-tribe of Menasheh in Bashan, thirteen cities.

7 The Merarites according to their clans received from the tribe of Reuven, the tribe of Gad, and the tribe of Zevulun, twelve cities.

8 These cities and their pasturelands the people of Yisra'EL gave by lot to the Levites, as YHWH had commanded through Moshe.

9 Out of the tribe of the people of Yehudah and the tribe of the people of Shim'on they gave the following cities mentioned by name,

10 Which went to the descendants of Aharon, one of the clans of the Kohathites who belonged to the people of Levi — for the lot fell to them first.

11 They gave them Kiryat-Arba — Arba being the father of Anak — that is, Khevron, in the hill country of Yehudah, along with the pasturelands around it.

12 But the fields of the city and its villages had been given to Kalev the son of Yefunneh as his possession.

13 And to the descendants of Aharon the priest they gave Khevron, the city of refuge for the manslayer, with its pasturelands, Livnah with its pasturelands,

14 Yattir with its pasturelands, Eshtemoa with its pasturelands,

15 Kholon with its pasturelands, Devir with its pasturelands,

16 Ayin with its pasturelands, Yuttah with its pasturelands, Beit-Shemesh with its pasturelands — nine cities out of these two tribes.

17 And out of the tribe of Binyamin, Giv'on with its pasturelands, Geva with its pasturelands,

18 Anatot with its pasturelands, and Almon with its pasturelands — four cities.

19 The cities of the descendants of Aharon, the priests, were in all thirteen cities with their pasturelands.

20 And the rest of the Kohathites belonging to the Kohathite clans of the Levites received their allotted cities from the tribe of Efrayim.

21 To them were given Shekhem, the city of refuge for the manslayer, with its pasturelands in the hill country of Efrayim, Gezer with its pasturelands,

22 Kivtzayim with its pasturelands, Beit-Kharon with its pasturelands — four cities.

23 And out of the tribe of Dan, Eltekeh with its pasturelands, Gibbeton with its pasturelands,

24 Ayalon with its pasturelands, Gat-Rimmon with its pasturelands — four cities.

25 And out of the half-tribe of Menasheh, Ta'anakh with its pasturelands, and Gat-Rimmon with its pasturelands — two cities.

26 The cities of the clans of the rest of the Kohathites were ten in all, with their pasturelands.

27 And to the Gershonites, one of the clans of the Levites, were given out of the half-tribe of Menasheh, Golan in Bashan with its pasturelands, the city of refuge for the manslayer, and Be-Eshterah with its pasturelands — two cities.

28 And out of the tribe of Yissaskhar, Kishyon with its pasturelands, Daberath with its pasturelands,

29 Yarmut with its pasturelands, En-Gannim with its pasturelands — four cities.

30 And out of the tribe of Asher, Mishal with its pasturelands, Avdon with its pasturelands,

31 Khelkat with its pasturelands, and Rekhov with its pasturelands — four cities.

32 And out of the tribe of Naftali, Kedesh in Galil with its pasturelands, the city of refuge for the manslayer, Khammot-Dor with its pasturelands, and Kartan with its pasturelands — three cities.

33 The cities of the several clans of the Gershonites were in all thirteen cities with their pasturelands.

34 And to the rest of the Levites, the Merarite clans, were given out of the tribe of Zevulun, Yokne'am with its pasturelands, Kartah with its pasturelands,

35 Dimnah with its pasturelands, Nahalal with its pasturelands — four cities.

36 And out of the tribe of Reuven, Betzer with its pasturelands, Yahatz with its pasturelands,

37 Kedemot with its pasturelands, and Mefa'at with its pasturelands — four cities.

38 And out of the tribe of Gad, Ramot in Gil'ad with its pasturelands, the city of refuge for the manslayer, Makhanayim with its pasturelands,

39 Kheshbon with its pasturelands, Ya'zer with its pasturelands — four cities in all.

40 As for the cities of the several Merarite clans, that is, the remainder of the clans of the Levites, those allotted to them were twelve cities in all.

41 The cities of the Levites in the midst of the possession of the people of Yisra'EL were in all forty-eight cities with their pasturelands.

42 These cities each had its pasturelands around it. So it was with all these cities.

43 Thus YHWH gave to Yisra'EL all the land that he swore to give to their fathers. And they took possession of it, and they settled there.

44 And YHWH gave them rest on every side just as he had sworn to their fathers. Not one of all their enemies had withstood them, for YHWH had given all their enemies into their hands.

45 Not one word of all the good promises that YHWH had made to the house of Yisra'EL had failed — all came to pass.

COMPARATIVE MANUSCRIPT NOTES

[DSS] 4QJosha (4Q47) preserves fragmentary portions of Chapter 21. The forty-eight Levitical cities distributed throughout all twelve tribal territories ensure that the covenant priestly presence is geographically dispersed throughout the entire land — no tribe is without Levitical teachers and sanctuary service nearby.

[ORIT GE'EZ] The Orit Ge'ez preserves Chapter 21 in full. Verse 43-45 — the great covenant summary — is read as the definitive declaration that YHWH's covenant with Avraham, Yitzhak, and Yaakov is fulfilled. Every promise kept. Every word standing.

[MASORETIC VARIANT] No significant variants from the DSS in Yehoshua Chapter 21. The Masoretic text aligns with the DSS reading. Verse 45 is one of the most theologically declarative verses in the entire Nevi'im corpus.

[CRITICAL NOTE] Verse 45 — not one word of all the good promises that YHWH had made to the house of Yisra'EL had failed — all came to pass. This is the covenant audit of the entire book of Yehoshua. Every promise YHWH made to Avraham in Bereshit 15 — the land, the nations driven out, the rest — is declared fulfilled. Zero failures. Zero partial fulfillments. All came to pass. The Hebrew lo nafal — not one fell — uses the image of a word falling to the ground unused and wasted. Not one of YHWH's words fell. Every word reached its mark. This verse is the theological climax of the entire conquest narrative.

YEHOSHUA'S FAREWELL — CHAPTERS 22 THROUGH 24

YEHOSHUA — CHAPTER 22 The Eastern Tribes Sent Home — The Altar by the Yarden — A Witness Between Us

1 At that time Yehoshua summoned the Reuvenites and the Gadites and the half-tribe of Menasheh,

2 And said to them, You have kept all that Moshe the servant of YHWH commanded you and have obeyed my voice in all that I have commanded you.

3 You have not forsaken your brothers these many days, down to this day, but have been careful to keep the charge of YHWH your Creator.

4 And now YHWH your Creator has given rest to your brothers, as he promised them. Therefore turn and go to your tents in the land where your possession lies, which Moshe the servant of YHWH gave you on the other side of the Yarden.

5 Only be very careful to observe the commandment and the Torah that Moshe the servant of YHWH commanded you, to love YHWH your Creator, and to walk in all his ways and to keep his commandments and to cling to him and to serve him with all your heart and with all your soul.

6 So Yehoshua blessed them and sent them away, and they went to their tents.

7 Now to the one half of the tribe of Menasheh Moshe had given a possession in Bashan, but to the other half Yehoshua had given a possession beside their brothers on the west side of the Yarden. And when Yehoshua sent them away to their homes and blessed them,

8 He said to them, Go back to your tents with much wealth and with very much livestock, with silver, gold, bronze, and iron, and with much clothing. Divide the spoil of your enemies with your brothers.

9 So the people of Reuven and the people of Gad and the half-tribe of Menasheh returned home, parting from the people of Yisra'EL at Shiloh, which is in the land of Kena'an, to go to the land of Gil'ad, their own land of which they had taken possession by command of YHWH through Moshe.

10 And when they came to the region of the Yarden that is in the land of Kena'an, the people of Reuven and the people of Gad and the half-tribe of Menasheh built there an altar by the Yarden, an altar of great appearance.

11 And the people of Yisra'EL heard it said, Behold, the people of Reuven and the people of Gad and the half-tribe of Menasheh have built the altar at the frontier of the land of Kena'an, in the region about the Yarden, on the side that belongs to the people of Yisra'EL.

12 And when the people of Yisra'EL heard of it, the whole assembly of the people of Yisra'EL gathered at Shiloh to make war against them.

13 Then the people of Yisra'EL sent to the people of Reuven and the people of Gad and the half-tribe of Menasheh, in the land of Gil'ad, Pinkhas the son of Ele'azar the priest,

14 And with him ten chiefs, one from each of the tribal families of Yisra'EL, every one of them the head of a family among the clans of Yisra'EL.

15 And they came to the people of Reuven, the people of Gad, and the half-tribe of Menasheh, in the land of Gil'ad, and they said to them,

16 Thus says the whole congregation of YHWH, What is this breach of faith that you have committed against the Creator of Yisra'EL in turning away this day from following YHWH by building yourselves an altar this day in rebellion against YHWH?

17 Have we not had enough of the sin at Pe'or from which even yet we have not cleansed ourselves, and for which there came a plague upon the congregation of YHWH,

18 That you too must turn away this day from following YHWH? And if you too rebel against YHWH today then tomorrow he will be angry with the whole congregation of Yisra'EL.

19 But now, if the land of your possession is unclean, pass over into YHWH's land where YHWH's tabernacle stands, and take for yourselves a possession among us. Only do not rebel against YHWH or make us rebels by building for yourselves an altar other than the altar of YHWH our Creator.

20 Did not Akhan the son of Zerakhi break faith in the matter of the devoted things, and wrath fell upon all the congregation of Yisra'EL? And he was not the only one who died for his iniquity.

21 Then the people of Reuven, the people of Gad, and the half-tribe of Menasheh said in answer to the heads of the families of Yisra'EL,

22 YHWH, Creator of creators, YHWH, Creator of creators — he knows — and Yisra'EL itself shall know. If it was in rebellion or in breach of faith against YHWH, do not spare us today

23 For building an altar to turn away from following YHWH. Or if we did so to offer burnt offerings or grain offerings or peace offerings on it, may YHWH himself take vengeance.

24 No, but we did it from fear that in time to come your children might say to our children, What have you to do with YHWH, the Creator of Yisra'EL?

25 For YHWH has made the Yarden a boundary between us and you, you people of Reuven and people of Gad. You have no portion in YHWH. So your children might make our children cease to worship YHWH.

26 Therefore we said, Let us now build an altar, not for burnt offering, nor for sacrifice,

27 But to be a witness between us and you, and between our generations after us, that we do perform the service of YHWH in his presence with our burnt offerings and sacrifices and peace offerings, so your children will not say to our children in time to come, You have no portion in YHWH.

28 And we thought, If this should be said to us or to our descendants in time to come, we should say, Behold, the copy of the altar of YHWH, which our fathers made, not for burnt offerings, nor for sacrifice, but to be a witness between us and you.

29 Far be it from us that we should rebel against YHWH and turn away this day from following YHWH by building an altar for burnt offering, grain offering, or sacrifice, other than the altar of YHWH our Creator that stands before his tabernacle.

30 When Pinkhas the priest and the chiefs of the congregation, the heads of the families of Yisra'EL who were with him, heard the words that the people of Reuven and the people of Gad and the people of Menasheh spoke, it was good in their eyes.

31 And Pinkhas the son of Ele'azar the priest said to the people of Reuven and the people of Gad and the people of Menasheh, Today we know that YHWH is in our midst, because you have not committed this breach of faith against YHWH. Now you have delivered the people of Yisra'EL from the hand of YHWH.

32 Then Pinkhas the son of Ele'azar the priest, and the chiefs, returned from the people of Reuven and the people of Gad in the land of Gil'ad to the land of Kena'an, to the people of Yisra'EL, and brought back word to them.

33 And the report was good in the eyes of the people of Yisra'EL. And the people of Yisra'EL blessed Creator and spoke no more of making war against them to destroy the land where the people of Reuven and the people of Gad were settled.

34 The people of Reuven and the people of Gad called the altar Witness, For, they said, it is a witness between us that YHWH is Creator.

COMPARATIVE MANUSCRIPT NOTES

[DSS] 4QJosha (4Q47) preserves fragmentary portions of Chapter 22. The altar crisis of Chapter 22 is the first major covenant conflict within Yisra'EL after the conquest. The covenant community nearly goes to civil war before the misunderstanding is resolved through covenant dialogue.

[ORIT GE'EZ] The Orit Ge'ez preserves Chapter 22 in full. Verse 22 — YHWH, Creator of creators, YHWH, Creator of creators — he knows — is read as the most solemn covenant oath in the entire book: the invocation of YHWH's complete knowledge as the ground of their declaration.

[MASORETIC VARIANT] No significant variants from the DSS in Yehoshua Chapter 22. The Masoretic text aligns with the DSS reading. The reference to Akhan in verse 20 — and he was not the only one who died for his iniquity — confirms the covenant principle of Chapter 7: individual sin produces communal consequence.

[CRITICAL NOTE] Verse 24-28 — the eastern tribes built the altar not for sacrifice but as a witness between generations. The problem they anticipated was covenant amnesia: in time to come your children might say to our children, what have you to do with YHWH? The altar is a memorial against the forgetting of covenant identity across the Yarden boundary. The same impulse as the twelve stones of Chapter 4. Covenant memory requires physical markers that generate questions. Verse 34 — they called the altar Witness, for it is a witness between us that YHWH is Creator. The altar is not for worship but for testimony.

YEHOSHUA — CHAPTER 23 Yehoshua's First Farewell Address — Be Strong to Keep the Torah — Cling to YHWH Your Creator

1 A long time afterward, when YHWH had given rest to Yisra'EL from all their surrounding enemies, and Yehoshua was old and well advanced in years,

2 Yehoshua summoned all Yisra'EL, its elders and heads, its judges and officers, and said to them, I am now old and well advanced in years.

3 And you have seen all that YHWH your Creator has done to all these nations for your sake, for it is YHWH your Creator who has fought for you.

4 Behold, I have allotted to you as an inheritance for your tribes those nations that remain, along with all the nations that I have already cut off, from the Yarden to the Great Sea in the west.

5 YHWH your Creator will push them back before you and drive them out of your sight. And you shall possess their land, just as YHWH your Creator promised you.

6 Therefore, be very strong to keep and to do all that is written in the Book of the Torah of Moshe, turning aside from it neither to the right hand nor to the left,

7 That you may not mix with these nations remaining among you or make mention of the names of their gods or swear by them or serve them or bow down to them,

8 But you shall cling to YHWH your Creator just as you have done to this day.

9 For YHWH has driven out before you great and strong nations. And as for you, no man has been able to withstand you to this day.

10 One man of you puts to flight a thousand, since it is YHWH your Creator who fights for you, just as he promised you.

11 Be very careful, therefore, to love YHWH your Creator.

12 For if you turn back and cling to the remnant of these nations remaining among you and make marriages with them, so that you associate with them and they with you,

13 Know for certain that YHWH your Creator will no longer drive out these nations before you, but they shall be a snare and a trap for you, a whip on your sides and thorns in your eyes, until you perish from off this good land that YHWH your Creator has given you.

14 And now I am about to go the way of all the earth, and you know in your hearts and souls, all of you, that not one word has failed of all the good things that YHWH your Creator promised concerning you. All have come to pass for you — not one of them has failed.

15 But just as all the good things that YHWH your Creator promised concerning you have been fulfilled for you, so YHWH will bring upon you all the evil things, until he has destroyed you from off this good land that YHWH your Creator has given you,

16 If you transgress the covenant of YHWH your Creator, which he commanded you, and go and serve other gods and bow down to them. Then the anger of YHWH will be kindled against you, and you shall perish quickly from off the good land that he has given you.

COMPARATIVE MANUSCRIPT NOTES

[DSS] 4QJosha (4Q47) preserves fragmentary portions of Chapter 23. Yehoshua's first farewell address rehearses the entire covenant framework: YHWH fought for you, not one word has failed, now cling to YHWH and keep the Torah or the covenant curses will come upon you.

[ORIT GE'EZ] The Orit Ge'ez preserves Chapter 23 in full. Verse 14 — not one word has failed of all the good things that YHWH your Creator promised — all have come to pass — is read as the covenant testimony that Yehoshua delivers at the end of his life: a lifetime of witnessing YHWH's faithfulness.

[MASORETIC VARIANT] No significant variants from the DSS in Yehoshua Chapter 23. The Masoretic text aligns with the DSS reading. Verse 15 is the covenant warning that mirrors the covenant promise: as every good word was fulfilled, so every judgment word will be fulfilled if the covenant is broken.

[CRITICAL NOTE] Verse 14 — not one word has failed of all the good things that YHWH your Creator promised — all have come to pass for you — not one of them has failed. This is the second declaration of zero covenant failures in Yehoshua, the first being 21:45. The repetition is the covenant testimony of a life: Yehoshua has watched YHWH's word prove true from the wilderness through the entire conquest. He is dying and this is his witness. Verse 15 immediately follows with the covenant bilateral: as every good word was fulfilled, every judgment word will also be fulfilled. YHWH's faithfulness to the covenant is not selective — it applies to both the blessings and the curses of Devarim 27-28.

YEHOSHUA — CHAPTER 24 The Covenant Renewal at Shekhem — Choose This Day Whom You Will Serve — As for Me and My House

1 Yehoshua gathered all the tribes of Yisra'EL to Shekhem and summoned the elders, the heads, the judges, and the officers of Yisra'EL. And they presented themselves before Creator.

2 And Yehoshua said to all the people, Thus says YHWH, the Creator of Yisra'EL, Long ago, your fathers lived beyond the Euphrates, Terakha, the father of Avraham and of Nakhor, and they served other gods.

3 Then I took your father Avraham from beyond the River and led him through all the land of Kena'an, and made his offspring many. I gave him Yitzhak.

4 And to Yitzhak I gave Yaakov and Esav. And I gave Esav the hill country of Se'ir to possess, but Yaakov and his children went down to Egypt.

5 And I sent Moshe and Aharon, and I plagued Egypt with what I did in the midst of it, and afterward I brought you out.

6 Then I brought your fathers out of Egypt, and you came to the sea. And the Egyptians pursued your fathers with chariots and horsemen to the Red Sea.

7 And when they cried to YHWH, he put darkness between you and the Egyptians and made the sea come upon them and cover them. And your eyes saw what I did in Egypt. And you lived in the wilderness a long time.

8 Then I brought you to the land of the Amorites, who lived on the other side of the Yarden. They fought with you, and I gave them into your hand, and you took possession of their land, and I destroyed them before you.

9 Then Balak the son of Tzippor, king of Moav, arose and fought against Yisra'EL. And he sent and invited Bil'am the son of Be'or to curse you,

10 But I would not listen to Bil'am. Indeed, he blessed you. So I delivered you out of his hand.

11 And you went over the Yarden and came to Yerikho, and the leaders of Yerikho fought against you, and also the Amorites, the Perizzites, the Kena'anites, the Khittites, the Girgashites, the Khivvites, and the Yevusites. And I gave them into your hand.

12 And I sent the hornet before you, which drove them out before you, the two kings of the Amorites — it was not by your sword or by your bow.

13 I gave you a land on which you had not labored and cities that you had not built, and you dwell in them. You eat the fruit of vineyards and olive orchards that you did not plant.

14 Now therefore fear YHWH and serve him in sincerity and in faithfulness. Put away the gods that your fathers served beyond the River and in Egypt, and serve YHWH.

15 And if it is evil in your eyes to serve YHWH, choose this day whom you will serve, whether the gods your fathers served in the region beyond the River, or the gods of the Amorites in whose land you dwell. But as for me and my house, we will serve YHWH.

16 Then the people answered, Far be it from us that we should forsake YHWH to serve other gods,

17 For it is YHWH our Creator who brought us and our fathers up from the land of Egypt, out of the house of slavery, and who did those great signs in our sight and preserved us in all the way that we went, and among all the peoples through whom we passed.

18 And YHWH drove out before us all the peoples, the Amorites who lived in the land. Therefore we also will serve YHWH, for he is our Creator.

19 But Yehoshua said to the people, You are not able to serve YHWH, for he is a holy Creator. He is a jealous Creator — he will not forgive your transgressions or your sins.

20 If you forsake YHWH and serve foreign gods, then he will turn and do you harm and consume you, after having done you good.

21 And the people said to Yehoshua, No, but we will serve YHWH.

22 Then Yehoshua said to the people, You are witnesses against yourselves that you have chosen YHWH, to serve him. And they said, We are witnesses.

23 He said, Then put away the foreign gods that are among you, and incline your heart to YHWH, the Creator of Yisra'EL.

24 And the people said to Yehoshua, YHWH our Creator we will serve, and his voice we will obey.

25 So Yehoshua made a covenant with the people that day, and put in place statutes and rules for them at Shekhem.

26 And Yehoshua wrote these words in the Book of the Torah of Creator. And he took a large stone and set it up there under the terebinth that was by the sanctuary of YHWH.

27 And Yehoshua said to all the people, Behold, this stone shall be a witness against us, for it has heard all the words of YHWH that he spoke to us. Therefore it shall be a witness against you, lest you deal falsely with your Creator.

28 So Yehoshua sent the people away, every man to his inheritance.

29 After these things Yehoshua the son of Nun, the servant of YHWH, died, being 110 years old.

30 And they buried him in his own inheritance at Timnat-Serakha, which is in the hill country of Efrayim, north of the mountain of Ga'ash.

31 Yisra'EL served YHWH all the days of Yehoshua, and all the days of the elders who outlived Yehoshua and had known all the work that YHWH did for Yisra'EL.

32 As for the bones of Yosef, which the people of Yisra'EL brought up from Egypt, they buried them at Shekhem, in the piece of land that Yaakov bought from the sons of Khamor the father of Shekhem for a hundred pieces of money. It became an inheritance of the descendants of Yosef.

33 And Ele'azar the son of Aharon died, and they buried him at Giv'at-Pinkhas, which had been given to Pinkhas his son in the hill country of Efrayim.

COMPARATIVE MANUSCRIPT NOTES

[DSS] 4QJosha (4Q47) preserves portions of Chapter 24. The covenant renewal at Shekhem is one of the most formally structured covenant ceremonies in the entire Nevi'im. It follows the classic ancient covenant treaty pattern: historical prologue — verses 2-13 — covenant stipulations — verses 14-15 — covenant response — verses 16-24 — covenant ratification — verses 25-27.

[ORIT GE'EZ] The Orit Ge'ez preserves Chapter 24 in full. Verse 15 — as for me and my house, we will serve YHWH — is one of the most recognized covenant declarations in the Beta Israel tradition: the individual covenant commitment made publicly and on behalf of the household.

[MASORETIC VARIANT] No significant variants from the DSS in Yehoshua Chapter 24. The Masoretic text aligns with the DSS reading. The burial of Yosef's bones at Shekhem — verse 32 — fulfills the oath sworn by Yosef in Bereshit 50:25 and carried by Yisra'EL through the entire Exodus and conquest — Shemot 13:19.

[CRITICAL NOTE] Verse 19-20 — Yehoshua said to the people, You are not able to serve YHWH. This is one of the most startling statements in the entire covenant literature: the covenant leader tells the people who have just pledged covenant faithfulness that they cannot keep their own pledge. He is not discouraging them — he is stripping away the presumption of easy covenant loyalty. YHWH is holy and jealous — he will not overlook casual covenant breach. The people confirm their pledge twice more — verses 21 and 24 — and Yehoshua accepts it. Verse 26-27 — Yehoshua wrote these words in the Book of the Torah of Creator — and took a large stone as a witness. The stone has heard YHWH's words. It will testify against the people if they break the covenant. The inanimate creation is called as covenant witness — the same theology as the song of Moshe in Devarim 32:1 where heaven and earth are called as witnesses.

END OF PART THREE — CHAPTERS 16 THROUGH 24 — YEHOSHUA COMPLETE
